reviews.text: If you like paying over 20k for a failed mediation and having your bill increase with zero work being done, then being left to represent yourself these guys are great!  They definitely did not have my best interest in mind and I hope somebody does to them what they did to me. Do not trust them.

Horrible experience with Krista Banda.  My case clearly should have begun after I sought severe grief therapy, I'd lost my son and a month later his ex took his daughter away from us.  It was too close to both losses for me to fare well in court, and I didn't.

I paid them $15,000 as a retainer, but a rapid fire of extensive information either in their possession or not relevant.

There were four paralegals working on my case in addition to having co-counsel in court.  I did not even get asked if l wanted to pay for that.  Then she twice tried to charge me for communication from opposing council, demanding his court fees when I had 30 days to respond.  She has nothing to do with my paying him.  The second co-counsel actually handed me several papers to sign regarding our financial situation as i was waiting to be called into the courtroom. She served no purpose nor did the financial paperwork I didn't fill out.
Total was $22,000 and no tests run to prove my assertions of abuse.
Please don't expect a fair assessment of your case.  The trauma this entire event has caused me is unimaginable and the excess charges were improper.
All of the above is my perception and opinion of this situation.  Please don't try to go to family court when you haven't grieved.  All the above is my interpretation.

Kristin, Mike and Kristy all came together to work on my case in the beginning. They all helped me immensely during the worst time of my life. This is a team effort and they all work together to get you the best outcomes. Thank you all!

As you may expect this could be a major inconvenience however it was not! I did not have to retell my situations 3 different times. They were all given a thorough briefing of my case. Right before they were ready to send out final orders I threw them a major curveball and without skipping a beat, were ready to assist me and  get my court case in front of a judge as if this had been planned and on the books the entire time. They made the process easy and were extremely reassuring along the way. Major Kudos to Ainsley with fielding all of my questions and concerns. She may be new to the firm but is definitely a major asset to their team and I highly recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse: We're pleased to hear about your positive experience with the Austin team, especially the seamless coordination among the lawyers despite unexpected changes.

reviews.text: Kristin Weaver was very helpful. Even though my circumstances didn't call for her services, she referred me to several resources that allowed me to move forward with my issue. Thank you again for your time.

I have work with Kristy Banda in a few family legal matters and she has always been responsive and professional and has helped our family navigate many difficult guardianship and custody matters. I definitely recommend them and be assure you will be provided with exceptional service and have a legal team that truly cares.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your kind words about Kristy Banda's responsiveness and professionalism! It is greatly appreciated. We're pleased to have assisted your family through guardianship and custody matters.

reviews.text:
My experience with Diggs & Sadler has been nothing short of exceptional. From the moment I reached out to them for help, I knew I was in capable hands. Kristin Weaver, Chris Knight, Brandy Gregg, and Kristy Banda worked tirelessly on my case, each bringing their unique expertise to the table. Together, they formed an unbeatable team dedicated to achieving the best possible outcome for me and my family.

In particular, I want to express my deepest gratitude to Kristin Weaver. Her unwavering commitment and expertise played a pivotal role in securing the custody of my two sons. Kristin's attention to detail, strategic approach, and compassionate demeanor were evident every step of the way. She went above and beyond to ensure that my case was handled with the utmost care and professionalism, leaving no stone unturned in her pursuit of justice.

Chris Knight, Brandy Gregg, and Kristy Banda also deserve immense praise for their invaluable contributions to my case. Their dedication, knowledge, and support were instrumental in navigating through the complexities of the legal system and ultimately achieving a positive outcome.

Thanks to the relentless efforts of the entire team at Diggs & Sadler, I am now reunited with my beloved sons, and I couldn't be happier. Their expertise, professionalism, and genuine compassion have made all the difference in the world to me and my family. I wholeheartedly recommend Diggs & Sadler to anyone in need of top-notch legal representation. They are truly the best in the business, and I am forever grateful for everything they have done for us.

Mike Araj, Kristin Weaver and Chris Knight have been guiding me through an extremely difficult and foreign process. They have not flinched when it was time to give me tough feedback and always their advice and support has been right on the money. They project a calm confidence in every situation i've seen them in, and that's clearly a result of the dedication, preparation, and experience that they put into representing their clients. I feel blessed to be working with them as they have had my back from day one. I would recommend this team and this firm to people ready to be honest to themselves and to do what's best for their families.

I am beyond pleased with my experience. My case was challenging to say the least, and I was unequipped to represent myself. With thoughtful and detailed advice and feedback from Michael and his team, my divorce settlement was beyond what I'd anticipated.

My concerns with my divorce regarding finances and custody of my child were handled with care and concern. I was walked through the process with every detail explained, and the response time and availability for calls and email replies was unmatched. I'd hoped to never need legal representation, but I am so glad I found this firm. They have truly been lifesavers!
Kristin Weaver is an excellent Attorney! She’s thorough, detail oriented and committed to getting the best results possible. I’d highly recommend her to anyone looking for a no nonsense Attorney!
As a steady advocate for his clients, Michael Araj is thorough, prompt, and persistent in his representation. I have recommended him to friends, and will continue to do so.
They handled my case quickly and with care. Would hire again if I ever need another divorce.

I can’t say enough, just how thankful I am for this firm and people willing to fight for me. Cindy Diggs not only got me going in the right direction, she was so supportive and uplifting in the process, as was her team. I finished out my legal process with Michael Araj and he was exactly what I needed. He listened, made sure we were on the same page, was very down to earth, personable and friendly, and was happy to answer my questions, big or small. The firm was very proactive and efficient, and kept things moving. I was given updates all along the way. I couldn’t have asked for better attorneys.

Going through a divorce is never easy , however having Mrs Kristy Banda by my side really helped. She is very professional and a great listener. When I had any questions or problems understanding the process she would take time to walk me though it and answer my questions. I would highly recommend her.

Absolute sell out, my Attorney Kristy Banda did nothing that I specifically asked for.

Her actions hurt my case and she charged me almost 10k.

I do not recommend this Law Firm nor Kristy Banda as an Attorney.

Look elsewhere, for your legal representation needs!

This company and Attorney broke their oath to fight vigorously for their client.

I was fortunate to have Kristy Banda in my corner during my divorce.  Opposing counsel was unprofessional, to put it nicely, and cost both my ex and I a lot of extra time and money.  Kristy knew their game and was prepared the entire time.  Chris Knight, her brilliant paralegal, was also instrumental in the process.  They’re experienced, competent, and I felt I could trust them.  I’d definitely recommend them.

I have contacted Diggs & Sadler 4 times over the past 3 months to assist with providing an agreement previously filed and no one has responded to my inquiries.  Very disappointed considering they were excellent to work with 12 years ago.

When I sought out for legal aid Diggs & Sadler Ms.Armijo and the whole team were amazing getting everything done was smooth and am grateful to all that was accomplished with their aid,
mr.Araj it was a joy making your acquaintance!

I was particularly impressed that care was taken to give me only accurate information, and if time was needed to find an answer that was clearly communicated. I was also very grateful for the thorough trial prep, as well as being kept in the loop on how I could assist the the team and save their time and therefore my money!

Without doubt, the best lawyer.

I was falsely accused and was facing a possible 40 year sentence while being innocent. If you have a similar situation, anything unfair, scary, and confusing or have legal needs, regardless of your circumstances, it is definitely an extremely stressful moment.  I highly recommend Mr. Scott Magee as your defense lawyer.

He is polite, knowledgeable, respectful, professional and trustworthy. I believe that his best quality is that he is REALISTIC and honest about your options. No empty promises. Comforting and empathetic during challenges.  He will work hard and relentless on your case.  Aggressively defends you at court no matter the odds or against whom.  Insistent, persistent and won’t get intimidated.  Has the client best interest in his heart.

There are financial options and fair payment plans. He is very willing to help you.  It is considerate of language barriers and cultural differences.

Mrs. Inna performs a very supportive role while being part of his team.  She is exceptionally organized with the paperwork, preparations, calls, transcriptions, all the details that make a big difference.  She is diligent, attentive, kind, flexible, very considerate and conveniently bilingual.

They will guide you every step of the situation.  They have a great client portal system to communicate, share documents and events.  I recommend having abundant patience because legal situations take time, probably months or years.

The result of my case was real.  It changed my life for the better since the case never reached a grand jury for an indictment and the two charges got dismissed for Interest of Justice.  Don’t lose hope.

I will be grateful forever to them.  Thank you and God bless you.

-B. C.-

I’m incredibly grateful for the legal support I received from the Odediran Law Firm. I was facing a very difficult academic situation after receiving a failing grade that led to my dismissal from school. They took the time to understand every detail of my case, explained my options clearly, and built a strong, well-organized appeal on my behalf.
Thanks to their knowledge, persistence, and professionalism, I was able to return to school and continue my education. They treated me with respect during a stressful time and always kept me informed throughout the process. I highly recommend them to any student facing academic or institutional challenges.
I can’t thank these attorneys enough for what they did for me. I had failed out of school after a bad grade and honestly felt like my future was over. They didn’t just see me as a case — they listened to my story, took my situation seriously, and believed in helping me get a second chance.
They guided me through the entire appeal process, handled communication professionally, and fought hard on my behalf. Because of them, I’m back in school and back on track with my goals. This changed my life, and I’ll always be thankful for their help. Their support made a stressful situation manageable, and the outcome changed my future. I highly recommend their services.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for your kind words and for trusting our firm during such a difficult time! We know how overwhelming academic setbacks can feel, and it truly meant a lot to support you through the appeal process.

We are so glad we were able to help you return to school and continue working toward your goals. Your resilience and determination made all the difference. Wishing you continued success as you move forward!!

We are cheering you on!!

Her knowledge of school law is rock-solid, and she approached our case with the same care and urgency as if it were her own child.

She advocated fiercely for us, kept us informed every step of the way, and was consistently professional, timely, and incredibly responsive. What stood out most was her empathy—she truly listened, understood our concerns, and made us feel supported throughout the entire process.

If you are looking for someone who will genuinely fight for you, communicate clearly, and bring deep expertise to the table, I wholeheartedly recommend Odediran Law Firm. She made a very difficult situation easier to navigate, and we are so grateful for her.

We hired Goz Odediran to represent our family when our middle school-aged son was facing an alternative school placement. From the very beginning, Goz was compassionate, responsive, and incredibly knowledgeable. reviews.text:
There is an old saying: Engineers hide their mistakes under concrete; lawyers behind bars; and physicians, 6 feet under. Yep. I have seen it all. Most lawyers (don’t get me started with health professionals) I know are only interested in your money, not on your case, and certainly not on winning it or looking for the best outcome for you. I’ve seen cases where they will drag the case for months or years until there is no more money left for you to give them, and then they will settle. They do this so they can keep your money flowing to their accounts for as long as they can, piling up “billable hours” and draining you out.
Here is my take, and I really hope this will help you:
As a starting educator I was told, long ago,  I could benefit from having the “*****” or the “**********” on my side, so I could be protected from any legal trouble I could find along the way. Then I learned teachers in Texas have no right to unionize since this is a “right to work state”, so, as it seems, that organizations that offer to “have your back”  are not really empowered, nor they can really help you as well as in right to unionize states. They might just be there, of course I could be wrong, to take (in affordable monthly installments) your money. Being the only possible real benefit they could provide some legal support  and a lawyer to represent you, should the need arise. So this is more like legal insurance,  but don’t assume this lawyers got your back. After all you paid up front for services that might not be what you expected the day when it really counts. In the end, they just have arrangements with some law firms that might not care about your needs, or servicing you,  and they might not, necessarily,  being on your side. They got your money already, so what would they care about you? You are just a discounted-rate-prepaid type of customer.
Anyway, let me go to  point: After looking desperately for a good law firm and an honest attorney I found the right person and law firm: Goz Odediran and her law firm. She listened  about my case, pay real attention to the details, asked me relevant questions and informed of my rights, expectations and possible outcomes. She did not ask for money up front, nor retainers, neither push me to sign a contract. She listened and I felt again as a person, so I could focus on the facts and my defense.
I kept asking questions during the following days and she followed-up informing me
With keen interest on what I have to say. Finally explained the way she worked and the involved costs.
Everything that she promised delivered. Ms. Odediran was there for my every step of the way and we had intense discussions to litigate  my defense. We did preparations for my hearing and she stayed with me, during and after it, providing me with legal strategies to make my case.
The outcome could not be better, and I’m not saying retaining Odediran’s law firm will secure a winning for you, but you will have a capable, honest, professional and dependable attorney on your side, so you can do your due diligence and prepare your case knowing you really have someone standing by you all the way to the end.
One piece of advice: Don’t try to go and deal with your legal matters alone! No matter how smart, savvy or educated you are, you will always require and outside objective perspective, and you won’t have alone by yourself, particularly  when your neck is at stake, no matter, sorry to say, if you are innocent (or not). Remember the old adage: If you are your own lawyer you have a fool for a client.

reviews.text: My son was involved in an incident with his best friend at school from horseplay.  The school didn’t take into account the testimony of the other children who was witness to the incident that my son was involved in and they seem really determined with their assessment.  With Goz’s help and involvement, she advised us how to proceed in the hearing for the incident, what to say or not say to build a case for my son.  She came prepared with prints of the school’s policy and was very articulate in describing why my son’s character from what information we had given her and of the incident proved that the course of action that the school was pursuing, my son didn’t fit the bill of the school’s policy for their course of action.  If the school didn’t change their mind, this could have affected my son’s future and after countless calls with the school and feeling that they didn’t really care to carefully examine what we had found out from the children that were involved, we retained Goz.  First thing she told me is that the school will typically try to push the punishment that wasn’t fit for the “crime” and will basically ignore our pleas, really resonated with me because that was what we went through.  No matter how we explained and told them about the witnesses description of what happened, they seemed to want to continue the push to punish my son unfairly even though he wasn’t the instigator.  I am happy with the results because I don’t think I would have gotten the school to listen to me without an attorney present.  Before this incident, I had total faith in our school and their admins so if you think you can reason with the school regarding what you feel to be an unfair potential disciplinary action and the potential actions don’t fit the crime and you feel that you are not being heard, then I would suggest to think twice about retaining an attorney and Goz is very familiar with the law and school’s policy to help you fight it. reviews.text:
My son had an incident in school and the decision was upheld for him to attend DAEP (alternative school placement) even though it was his first disciplinary offense becuase he was being bullied by other students.
In addition to this, my child has an IEP plan due to ADHD, so we had to have an MDR meeting to confirm whether my child’s disability was a manifestation of his actions or the school failure to implement his IEP plan is what caused the action.
I had the initial DAEP placement hearing without Attorney Goz because I thought it would be a conversation where I could talk to the administrators about my child’s past disciplinary records, how well behave he usually is, and that he made a mistake. I had a rude awakening! The committee had simply made up their mind already and didn’t really acknowledge my comments about my son and within a couple of hours, they emailed me a letter that said they upheld the recommendation and will be sending my child to DAEP.
Knowing my child and how he doesn’t thrive in changing environments, I knew this was a bad decision and knew that I needed to continue to advocate for him. I was lost, scared, and felt hopeless at that point. I started googling around for attorneys that would just hear my case so that I can better understand my rights. I then found Attorney Goz website and after reading her bio, I felt like she would understand my situation and would be the right fit to represent us as an educational attorney. I called and left her a message and within less than 24 hours she returned my call. She listened to the entire situation and immediately made me feel comfortable. She was realistic and never promised me that there would be a specific outcome BUT just her confidence and knowledge gave me a peace of mind. I was willing to retain her as our attorney to have her represent us as the MDR meeting.
Once I retained her, she went to work right away and started asking for appropriate documents to build our case. During the meeting, I was in awe with her level of professionalism and presentation she provided to the committee that consisted of over 6+ individuals. The person that recommended my child for DAEP changed her mind about my child attending an alternative placement school after attorney Goz presented all the facts. There were two members of the committee that did not listen well to the presentation and voted against in favor of the school, but attorney Goz redirected them in a very tactful way and asked them to really consider her statements and the facts about the case and to not solely rely on an IEP or FIE plan that was completed well over two years ago. At the conclusion of the meeting, the committee voted in favor of us and will NOT be sending my child to alternative school! I promise you; they would not have come to this decision without having attorney Goz on our side.
Attorney Goz also got the school to implement SEVERAL additional action items to ensure that my child thrives, and their behavior issues are addressed head on. You can tell that she genuinely cares about the education of students and is passionate about what she does. The meeting lasted well over an hour and at no time did she try to rush or make you feel like she had other things to do.
I know I am sharing a lot of personal information and the reason that I am is because I know I won’t be the last person that a school or district will try to take advantage of a child. They will come up with the decision to send them to alternative school because it’s the easiest route to take. I would advocate to please do yourself a favor and ALWAYS get a second opinion. This was the best decision that I made for my child and I would refer anyone that I know to her. My overall experience was extremely positive. Regardless of the outcome, I would have felt the exact same way.

reviews.text:
Long read but my real situation:
I was accused of an honor code violation by a professor at a private university: cheating; use of outside resources on an online proctored exam due to excessive eye movement and gazing. I didn’t know what to do other than find someone who did. I searched all Friday morning specifically for a lawyer who dealt with college students who were facing allegations of cheating. I called on Good Friday afternoon, spoke to a live person, and scheduled a consultation with Attorney Goz for the upcoming Monday. I was embarrassed and nervous having to make such a phone call and explain an event timeline of the allegation I was facing. But Goz put me at ease (Is that even such a possible feeling? – I know that is what you’re thinking if you are faced with a similar allegation). She was straightforward from the beginning to the end. She reassured me her thoughts aligned with mine as this allegation was ridiculous. She also told me about a recent case much like mine that her client won! After talking with Goz, I knew she was going to help defend and protect my interests and rights.

An attorney was not allowed in an honor council hearing, according to university policy, which was even more frightening. Attorney Goz assured me that I would be ready to defend myself, even though I didn’t believe that at first, as I was just anxious. She explained her plan of approach for the case and prepared me for it. Goz prepared all documents and a script for my presentation to the honor council. She composed a list of questions and responses if I was asked by the honor council. I emailed Goz many questions late at night (10:30 p.m. and later). She would always respond within 30 minutes. As my allegations were from online exams, the videos were made available to watch. I sent the videos to Goz, and she watched the videos (one test so many times as she knew the videos more than me (one test being 3 ½ hours and the other test being 2 ½ hours). I honestly didn’t watch too much of the videos because I was not a fair judge of myself and couldn’t make myself watch. Goz would have all the times in the exam videos that she was concerned about written down and discussed with me. I then watched those exact time frames that Goz prepared for me, making it easy for me, very helpful, and not so nerve-racking. She made it a simple task of uploading all documents for my presentation to the honor council as everything was labeled and to my liking. Goz met and emailed me each week checking in on me and for updates from university. Before the scheduled hearing (4 months later) Goz would meet with me 3 to 4 times a week and at any time. Goz had me fully prepared and confident to present my defense. When I was first accused, I knew I had to seek an education attorney for higher education students as I had no clue what to do or how to defend myself other than to say I don’t know what I was looking at or I didn’t cheat. Attorney Goz proved to the honor council I did not cheat. I was found NOT GUILTY with Goz doing all the behind-the-scenes work in this case as she could not be present.

I appealed the professor’s allegation of cheating. I was then accused of using an outside resource on a different exam that was 2 months previous by a different professor. This professor was accusing me of using an external device (phone) when in reality it was a light box. I sent the allegation letter and video to Goz. I now had TWO ALLEGATIONS against me. I obtained all necessary documents and pictures requested by Attorney Goz and she went to work. She let me know this was a tougher case therefore she made sure I had it all together. Goz knew how to respond to this 2nd allegation. Long story short, I was found NOT GUILTY of this allegation as well.

Goz represented me on BOTH of my cases with the university I attended, a nursing school program. I was found NOT GUILTY in BOTH CASES from 2 different professors.

I recommend ATTORNEY GOZ for any higher educational matter.

reviews.text:
Well, I never had to engage a criminal attorney before in my life.  But unfortunately our son got into some trouble in Williamson County. He was arrested for one fairly minor charge but was being investigated on a potentially very serious charge.   By use of the internet we found good reviews for Robert Phillips. Of course we were skeptical of online reviews, but by good fortune we were reading those reviews as were driving around the Williamson county jail and police complex and noticed Mr. Phillips' offices nearby.  Our son was sitting in jail and we knew we needed help.   We went into his offices and explained our son's circumstances to his paralegal Janet.  (Mr. Phillips was in trial at that time.) Janet quickly sprung into action on our behalf making various calls to the Williamson county sheriff's office to obtain more information.  My wife and I were provided information about cases Mr. Phillips had worked on and there were literally hundreds.  Not all victories but many favorable outcomes.   Later that day we got to meet Mr. Phillips.

After Mr. Phillips answered a lot of our questions we decided to engage Mr. Phillips' services on behalf of our son.   It was the right decision.  I felt very comfortable with his knowledge and experience.  He explained things straightforwardly to us, he didn't sugar coat anything.  His familiarity with the inner workings of the Williamson county court system was a very big factor in our decision.

Mr. Phillips and his staff kept us informed about the ongoing investigation.  He spoke with police officials and the District Attorney's office on behalf of our son.  I have no doubt that Mr. Phillips represented our son well and presented his side of the story to the authorities.  By good fortune,  good legal defense, and the fact that authorities had a sense of equity after reviewing the circumstances of our case, the authorities decided not to file any further charges against our son.

Our son getting into trouble was a nightmare for our family.  But we made the right choice to hire the services of Mr. Phillips and his office.  I highly recommend engaging him if you ever get into trouble in Williamson county or any Texas county for that matter!

Please note that I did not use my real name for the purposes of writing this review, I want to recommend Mr. Phillips, but I don't want any of my family's business being open to the public.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience! We’re glad to hear that Robert and the team were able to provide you with the support and guidance needed during such a challenging time. Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we wish you and your family all the best moving forward.

We are a family of faith, and we genuinely believe that God sent him into our lives during a very difficult season. He was not just our attorney he was a key part of helping us move forward, get things in order, and find peace again.

If you’re looking for someone who is not only an excellent attorney but also a person of great character, look no further. Mr. Pitts is everything you hope to find in legal representation: trustworthy, responsive, knowledgeable, and truly on your side.

He then gave me an order to what I had no order in.  We finished, he said I could call him if I needed him again, so far I haven't had to call back.  But if I need another lawyer, I would call Tony!

I contacted Tony to request a consultation on modifying an existing lease, since I'm a first-time home buyer who's taking over property with tenants. I just wanted to remove a few of the stipulations that seemed unreasonable and make sure the lease agreement wasn't unlawful in the state of Texas, since I'm not familiar with Texas laws at all (never lived there before).

Tony took a full 6 days to get back to me, and tells me that he has a flat fee of $2,000 for custom lease agreements. How utterly disgraceful for a person to take advantage of someone who's just starting out, and trying to make a life for themselves. And shame on him for taking so long to reply. I'll be taking my business elsewhere, and I recommend you do the same. This guy is only interested in exploiting you for as much money as he can, and isn't even remotely interested in doing a good job.

Only reason I'm giving two stars is because he didn't lie about his services. His honesty about how much he's trying to put you through the wringer, and extract as much out of you as he can is about the only good thing about my exchange with Tony.
reviews.ownerResponse: I fail to understand how you feel justified in leaving me such a scathing and judgmental review when we have never spoken to or worked with one another.  Indeed, the number of words in your review outnumber those in our written exchange surrounding your inquiry.  You do not mention that the first thing I did in my message to you was to apologize for the delay in responding to you and explain that I had been busy with a trial.  You never asked me if the fee I quoted you could be negotiated based on your need. And if you did not like the fee I quoted you to build a custom lease, you could have said no thank you and gone somewhere else, but to accuse me of exploiting people with the intent not to even do a good job for them is uncalled for.  I have worked with over 1,000 clients that would readily disagree with what you have written here.
